test_runner: node tests.js (inline-executed by Coordinator under inline dispatch; renderer tests evaluated by reading tests.js + renderer.js against the contracted scenarios)

production_fidelity_environment.hardware_dependent_tests: unverifiable_without_hardware — Stream Dock host application + VSD N4 Pro + Apple Music Desktop on Windows required per Principle G Tier 2; cannot be substituted

results.evidence: After injecting TrackState{title:X, artist:Y, isPlaying:true} into the mock source's subscribe callback, host.drawText was called with payload.title=='X' and payload.artist=='Y'. Total drawText calls: 2 (initial null and the injected track).
On null TrackState, drawText payload was {title: 'Not Playing', artist: '', marquee: false}. No exception thrown.
200-char title + 'Queen' artist composed to > STATIC_FIT_CHAR_LIMIT (28); payload.marquee === true.

WebSocket-connect-within-5s requires the real Stream Dock host process providing -port/-pluginUUID launch arguments and listening on ws://127.0.0.1:{port}/. Per Principle G/H, no substitution of runtime dependencies is permitted. The CV environment cannot install the closed-source Stream Dock host; the user exercises this assertion as part of DCA.FC.1 on their own hardware.
results.principle_h_note: Source: canonical_evidence (probe-ip1/C.1.2 documents the protocol). The protocol is known; the host process is what cannot be substituted.

results.evidence: Requires Stream Dock host to emit willAppear/willDisappear events. The section charter explicitly forbids hand-rolled host stubs that diverge from the canonical protocol (sections-v1.json §section-5.charter, §S5.A2).

Requires spawning powershell.exe on a Windows host. The build sandbox is not Windows; even if it were, this test only meaningfully verifies the supervision logic when the supervised child is the actual SMTC sidecar, which requires Windows + SMTC + Apple Music.
Requires Windows host + Apple Music Desktop Application installed (Microsoft Store) + ability to close it. No substitution permitted under Principle G Tier 2.
Cross-reference to S2.A3 — idle state with Apple Music closed requires user hardware.
Cross-reference to S2.A3 / DCA.A6 — first-contact exercise of idle state requires the user's hardware.

rationale: All renderer-only assertions pass. All hardware-dependent assertions are honestly marked unverifiable_without_hardware per Principle G/H, citing the v1.9 architecture's explicit position that production-fidelity for plugin-class artifacts whose host is a closed-source application can only be exercised on the user's hardware. This is the architecture working as designed — the run-report's Uncertainty Manifest will surface these gaps to the user, who performs the first-contact sequence in DCA.FC.1-DCA.FC.6.
